preamble: The reduction of [o] to [ə] is one of the most common vowel shifts in stress-timed languages. It occurs exclusively in unstressed syllables where the vowel loses its distinct quality and centralizes.

examples.note: Yoruba mid vowels /o/ and /e/ can reduce to more central values in unstressed or rapid speech; also relevant in ATR harmony alternation [+ATR] o vs [-ATR] ɔ.
Second and third 'o' are post-stress schwas.
Unstressed middle syllable reduces to schwa.

context: In unstressed syllables; reduction is conditioned by stress placement
allophones_note: In English: unstressed syllables; In Russian: akan'je (post-tonic [o] reduces to [ə] in unstressed position)
